Health
Solving health access through proximate innovation
We believe that innovators have an important role to play in improving health and well-being for all. The need is urgent, with
half the world lacking access to comprehensive health services and two billion people facing financial hardship due to out-of-pocket healthcare costs. New voices and ideas—from proximate innovators—can help address health disparities and make care more accessible, affordable, and sustainable.
Global Health Anchor Supporter
The Johnson & Johnson Foundation champions Solve's health innovators as they grow and scale; supporting their participation in Solve’s year-long skill-building program and providing unrestricted prize funding.
Meet the health innovators
Our health Solver teams have delivered health-related products and services to over 34 million patients

Impact Investing
Maximize your impact
The Solve Innovation Future fund is disrupting traditional philanthropic giving with direct catalytic investments in Solve’s early-stage innovators who are using technology to solve the world's most pressing problems.
The rigid expectations of the venture capital community are stifling opportunities for entrepreneurs, especially for those working on solving social problems with non traditional backgrounds. Solve Innovation Future allows these exceptional innovations to scale at their own pace.
Download the Solve Innovation Future Report“ Winning the MIT Solve award, followed by the investment from Solve Innovation Future, has been invaluable for EarlyBird. As an early-stage company, it granted us immediate credibility, which contributed to successful sales in the school market, winning awards and recruiting employees

Builders of bold solutions
The most transformative solutions often come from people who never intended to be innovators at all—they simply rejected broken systems. To date, we’ve supported 460 innovators that are reaching over 430 million people worldwide.
Meet the innovators
To solve world challenges, we need big, bold ideas. This is why we launch open innovation challenges each year in search of the best solutions using technology to solve intractable problems impacting communities locally and worldwide. Learn more about the status-quo-breaking ideas and the visionaries behind them

Builders of bold solutions
The most transformative solutions often come from people who never intended to be innovators at all—they simply rejected broken systems. To date, we’ve supported 460 innovators that are reaching over 430 million people worldwide.
Meet the innovators
To solve world challenges, we need big, bold ideas. This is why we launch open innovation challenges each year in search of the best solutions using technology to solve intractable problems impacting communities locally and worldwide. Learn more about the status-quo-breaking ideas and the visionaries behind them

Solver Spotlights
The innovators behind the innovations
Our Solver teams are dedicated to wielding technology for good. Whether it's an AI app teaching remote refugee children in Lebanon or affordable electric trucks connecting rural farmers in Africa to markets—their ideas and innovations are the heartbeat of our work.
Learn more about these leaders for change.

Become a Solver
Make connections, receive funding, scale the work, change the world
Are you an innovator, barrier-breaker, creator, or disrupter wielding technology for good? Well then, you just might be a Solver.
Each year, we launch an open call for solutions to our Global Challenges, focusing on tech-based innovations solving within climate, health, learning, economic prosperity, and Indigenous communities.
The best, brightest, and boldest ideas are selected and become Solvers— receiving funding, connections to our network, resources within MIT and beyond, and tailored support.
Solve's Global Challenges are open for submission from September to November each year in Solve’s five key areas: Climate, Learning, Health, Economic Prosperity, and Indigenous Communities.
Note: the information here relates to our annual Global Challenges

Challenges
Solving global problems with open innovation
In order to find and scale the best ideas to the most intractable issues of our time, we launch open innovation challenges. We built a research-backed platform and a proven methodology that seeks tech-based solutions from anyone, anywhere in the world. Our reach is vast, with tens of thousands of applicants from nearly every corner of the world.
And we’re not done yet.
-
100+challenges run to-date
-
28,200+solutions submitted
-
190+countries represented in submissions
Open and Upcoming Challenges
Past Challenges
